title: If Shinji was like this in EoE
display_title: If Shinji was like this in EoE
wikitext:
[]: If Shinji was like this in EoE             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Mon Jun 22, 2009 8:27 pm The previous topic on Shinji being universally hated was tugging in this direction. Now, let me give you an alternate EoE, with a slightly different Shinji. My question: Is this the way you'd rather see Shinji in EoE? Is it a more "acceptable" Shinji? A less "hateable" one? For a start, when the JSSDF attack begins, Misato finds Shinji a lot faster than before, and after shooting the guards dead, orders him to get to the Eva. Both of them stand beside the Eva with Shinji refusing to go in, and Misato annoyed at seeing the effect of her own order. Asuka discovers that Mama is in the Eva, and mother and daughter go on their rampage. The harpies strike, with Misato Shinji: I can't pilot it...not with the bakelite. Misato: I'll have to order charges set off...or use Eva-02... Asuka (On radio): No one will stop me, not with Mama here! Shinji: Mama? Mother? Asuka: I can't fight these monsters away forever. Misato: Shinji-kun...I'm sorry...if Asuka dies, it's my fault. Shinji: Asuka...die? *pause* Mother... Misato: Shinji? (Shinji steps forwards) Shinji: Mother? Mother! Mother, please...one last time...mother, if you don't move...if we just stay here...Asuka will die! We will all die! Mother, you've been protecting me all along. Mother, please! Please! One last time! *silence* Shinji: She doesn't... *Bakelite breaks apart, Eva-01's hand reaches out* Shinji then goes aboard Eva-01, and Yui takes control. The harpies get their butts double-whooped, but instrumentality then happens. Shinji lets people have their choice, with Seele getting tanged, Gendo being bitten by Yui-sama, and the souls of those who chose instrumentality launched into space with the Rei superbeing. Back on Earth, Asuka high-fives baka-Shinji and people rebuild their now Angel-free world.

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Mon Jun 22, 2009 8:42 pm *begins listing countless fanfics*

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Mon Jun 22, 2009 9:50 pm You pretty much wrote Shinji as he was in EP 19. While used (badly) in countless fanfics, that was well done. Of course, this being EVA, that's what we expected and wanted, but not what we got...

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Jun 22, 2009 10:13 pm The end result is similar, but we end up liking Shinji instead of hating him. That's more or less what I would have liked, instead of turning Shinji into a little ass who tries to strangle an unconscious girl.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jun 22, 2009 10:32 pm It would have been ok that way but I prefer the way things really went down. Though I did like that in your version Shinji gave people the choice to become part of instrumentality or to stay on earth.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Mon Jun 22, 2009 10:40 pm The first part perhaps, but I don't see how that last part with instrumentality could occur as it does in this scenario. with Shinji as you described, i don't see how Rei would get the cue to help him instead of gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Mon Jun 22, 2009 11:17 pm Yo, Mom Get up cause we got shit to do today! Come on, those birds called you a fatty! -moments later- ROAARR!

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Mon Jun 22, 2009 11:40 pm Zaque wrote: The first part perhaps, but I don't see how that last part with instrumentality could occur as it does in this scenario. with Shinji as you described, i don't see how Rei would get the cue to help him instead of gendo.

[]:             Quote Post by Zaque » Tue Jun 23, 2009 12:12 am I was referring to the part in the movie where Shinji does his whole yelling his head off thing after seeing eva02 torn apart, eaten by the harpies and the movie cuts to Rei down in terminal dogma 'noticing" that he's in trouble or at least a great deal of turmoil. with Asuka still alive and Shinji beating the harpies as well (there's also no real reason for yui to take control if Shinji was the one who demanded he pilot), it's rather difficult for me to imagine how there'd be Shinji's instrumentality. I would think that in this case, it would be more like gendo's intended version of instrumentality than anything else. the more i think about it, the more it seems to me that it was almost necessary for Shinji to behave the way he did for instrumentality to occur as it did and for the movie to have the same impact as it did. after all, the "one more final" was put in there for a reason, and if the movie ended more happily as was described here, I doubt that the message conveyed would be the same.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Jun 23, 2009 12:37 am Is it really so bad if the message ends up being different? Is the message that the actual ending tells really worth making Shinji into the universally bemoaned and disliked jerk that he has the reputation of? Because for all intents and purposes, it paints the picture that Shinji is a douche, but he will behave as long as there's the possibility of receiving affection. Personally, that's not the image of Shinji that I'd prefer to be left with, because it's a step backwards for his character. This discussion makes me very curious about how Rebuild is going to end... Is Anno just going to give us more of the same, or is he going to try and somehow restore Shinji's image to the fans? I'm quite curious to find out...

[]:             Quote Post by Combs » Tue Jun 23, 2009 12:54 am So in this scenario yui-sama has no plans concerning third impact? Though this Shinji is more relatable too, the ending would be stupid and i'd dislike it. I don't see how he'd have made any progress here. It wouldnt be as emotionally poweful as the original. Though he does take the role of the hero, i'd only like him better if he figured out he should do this on his own.

[]: Re: If Shinji was like this in EoE             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 23, 2009 1:37 am UrsusArctos wrote: ...a scenario not a million miles from my Asuka fic's in overall shape, apart from the names of some of the players in some of the roles...

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Tue Jun 23, 2009 5:04 am Maybe not quite like that (why is Misato blaming herself... and yes, Rei would probably side with Gendo, meaning we'd actually get the trans-human good ending) but otherwise, very yes.

[]:             Quote Post by UrsusArctos » Tue Jun 23, 2009 8:17 am Well, the whole idea of my fanfic ending was just to see what the real issue with Shinji was. Now, another possibility...since we don't get to see what happens in the meantime. Episode 24- Ritsuko: (Kaworu) is probably the final messenger. *Kaworu hijacks Eva-02 while singing the Ode to Joy* Misato: What? He's an Angel??? Now, this is just as bad a plothole as the one with the monoliths at the lake. Unless Kaworu and Misato both have Alzheimers, what may have happened? Misato: Bosh! He may be weird, but he can't be another Angel! He's human! No...something else is wrong here... Well, that doesn't sound right either, does it? But, wait...here's more... *Shinji is kidnapped and secretly exposed to the monoliths* Herr Lorenz: I find it remarkable that Major Katsuragi did not inform you that your best friend was an Angel. Shinji: But...she...what do you mean? Pierre (French guy): She visited Dr. Akagi in her holding location. It seems she wanted a second opinion on the Angel's identity. Herr Lorenz: She was well aware of Dr. Akagi's opinion that the child was the seventeenth Angel, yet she chose not to act. Or rather, she chose to leave the killing to you. Vlad (Russian member): She did so fully aware of your friendship. Shinji: No...she wanted me to kill him...to take revenge...? Had Seele manipulated Shinji the way they did Ritsuko...I don't know if my "bad fanfic" really fits the situation, but I assume that there would be trouble.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Tue Jun 23, 2009 1:08 pm The first scenario: The message of the entire series is radically altered and the series probably loses it's charm by becoming cliche feel-good bullshit with bitchin' action sequences and the POWR OF FRIENDSHIP. Anno is denied his "fuck you" to the world, and we don't get Rebuild because he totally wrote Shinji the way he did to reflect all otaku losers (this means YOUUUU lololol). The second scenario: Buttsex.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Tue Jun 23, 2009 1:10 pm AuraTwilight wrote: The first scenario: The message of the entire series is radically altered and the series probably loses it's charm by becoming cliche feel-good bullshit with bitchin' action sequences and the POWR OF FRIENDSHIP. Anno is denied his "fuck you" to the world, and we don't get Rebuild because he totally wrote Shinji the way he did to reflect all otaku losers (this means YOUUUU lololol). The second scenario: Buttsex.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Jun 23, 2009 2:08 pm AuraTwilight wrote: he message of the entire series is radically altered and the series probably loses it's charm

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Jun 23, 2009 2:44 pm I don't see how the series loses it's charm if we don't hate the main character when it's over. That seems like somewhat backwards logic to me.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jun 23, 2009 4:07 pm I'm quite happy with how Shinji was in EoE, because like Yojimbo said in the other thread, it WAS character development for the kid, although not exactly flattering character development. That aside... Ursus: Your idea is good, and while I could see Shinji going ep19 at first, I think that Rei would still defy Gendo (depending on just what would happen in such a scenario) and Instrumentality would still occur. It's possible that in a slightly alternate take of 3I/Instrumentality (assuming 3I still happens, not just Instrumentality. I recall hearing some....where that you don't need one for the other to occur. (EoTV???)), Seele could be tanged for good while the rest of humanity (including Gendo) could return if they want and blah blah blah. At least, those are my thoughts on the subject.

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Tue Jun 23, 2009 4:58 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: I'm quite happy with how Shinji was in EoE, because like Yojimbo said in the other thread, it WAS character development for the kid, although not exactly flattering character development.
